description: This bar chart compares the yield strength prediction errors (unit: MPa) of two machine learning models, Extra Trees (red gradient bars) and Genetic Algorithm optimized Extra Trees (GA-Extra Trees, blue gradient bars), for Fe-0.25C-3Mn-2Al-0.4Si medium-manganese low-density steel samples treated with cold rolling followed by annealing at 750℃, 800℃, and 850℃ respectively, plus tempering at 400℃. The x-axis represents annealing temperature, the y-axis is prediction error value in MPa. Explicit measured values are: for the 750℃ annealed sample, Extra Trees error = 34 MPa, GA-Extra Trees error = 12 MPa; for the 800℃ annealed sample, Extra Trees error = 31 MPa, GA-Extra Trees error = 10 MPa; for the 850℃ annealed sample, Extra Trees error = 36 MPa, GA-Extra Trees error = 19 MPa. The consistent significant reduction in prediction error across all tested samples demonstrates that GA optimization effectively improves the prediction accuracy of the Extra Trees model, supporting the validity of the developed GA-Extra Trees framework for high-precision yield strength prediction of medium-manganese low-density steels.
